zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian於1200年出生在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾時，他的父親William de Moravia of Petty36歲，母親Agnes Olifard31歲。他於大約1217年與Devergulla Olifard在英國蘇格蘭素斯 藍納可爾波維爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他於1226年被列為succeeded his father。他去世於1278年在蘇格蘭享年78歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭素斯 藍納可爾波維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian于1200年出生在苏格兰艾爾金爾时，他的父亲William de Moravia of Petty36岁，母亲Agnes Olifard31岁。他于大约1217年与Devergulla Olifard在英国苏格兰素斯 藍納可爾波維爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他于1226年被列为succeeded his father。他去世于1278年在苏格兰享年78岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰素斯 藍納可爾波維爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Moray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrey
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Morey  .

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moree
meanings: Altered form of   Morey  .
French (Morée): habitational name from a place so named in Loir-et-Cher.

variants.name: Morie
meanings: Altered form of Moree, itself perhaps an altered form of English or French Morey (compare 2 below) or of Irish Morrow .
English (London): variant of   Morey  . This surname is rare in Britain.
French: unexplained. This surname is rare in France.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Petty,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Petit  . The name is also found inIreland, the main branch there having been established in County Kerryin the 17th century by Sir William Petty.

variants.name: Pettey
meanings: English: variant of   Petty  .

variants.name: Pettay
meanings: English: altered form of   Petty  .

variants.name: Pettys
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Petty  .

variants.name: Pettee
meanings: English: altered form of   Petty  .

variants.name: Petit
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Michel, Normand, Mireille, Philippe, Pierre, Achille, Armand, Jean Claude, Jean Jacques, Ketly, Adrien.

French, Walloon, Catalan, English, Jewish (from southern France), West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from (Old) French, Catalan, and Middle English petit ‘small’, hence a nickname for a small man (or an ironic nickname for a big man), or a distinguishing name for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name. Compare Little , Petee , and Petite .

History: Charles Petit from Anneville-Ambourville in Seine-Maritime, France, married Jeanne Rossignol (Martin and Renée Desjardins) in Quebec City, QC, in 1670. Nicolas Petit dit Lapré/Laprée from Le Gué-d'Alleré in Charente-Maritime, France, married Marie Pouponnel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. Pierre Petit dit Gobin from Lyon in Rhône married Marguerite Véron in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1692. Pierre Petit dit Saint-Pierre from Évreux in Eure, France, married Judith Miville in Quebec City, QC, in 1758; see also St. Pierre . — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Lothian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the region so named in southeastern central Scotland. The placename is very ancient and is of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Louthan
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lothian  .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian was born in 1200, in Elginshire, Scotland, his father, William de Moravia of Petty, was 36 and his mother, Agnes Olifard, was 31. He married Devergulla Olifard about 1217, in Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. In 1226, at the age of 26, his occupation is listed as succeeded his father. He died in 1278, in Scotland, at the age of 78, and was buried in Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian est né en 1200, à Elginshire, Écosse, son père, William de Moravia of Petty, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Agnes Olifard, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Devergulla Olifard environ 1217, à Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. En 1226, à l’âge de 26 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est succeeded his father. Il est décédé en 1278, à Écosse, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.Bothwell Castle, Bothwell, South Lanarks: Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian 1200 in Elginshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Moravia of Petty 36 und seine Mutter Agnes Olifard 31. Er hat um 1217 in Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Devergulla Olifard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. 1226, als er 26 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als succeeded his father aufgeführt. Er starb 1278 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Scotland und wurde in Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian è nato il nel 1200 nel Elginshire, Scotland, il suo padre, William de Moravia of Petty, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Olifard, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Devergulla Olifard circa 1217, nel Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Nel 1226, all'età di 26 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come succeeded his father. Morì nel 1278, nel Scotland, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothianが1200年にスコットランドエルジンシュアイアで生まれた時，父William de Moravia of Pettyは36歳，母Agnes Olifardは31歳であった。約1217年にイギリススコットランドソウス ラナルクシュアイアボスウエルでDevergulla Olifardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。1226年，26歳の時，職業はsucceeded his fatherと記録されている。彼は1278年にスコットランドで78歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドソウス ラナルクシュアイアボスウエルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian(이)가 1200년 스컷랜드 엘긴실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Moravia of Petty(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Olifard(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1217년 영국 스코틀랜드 소우스 랜아르크실르 벗웰에서 Devergulla Olifard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 1226년, 26세의 나이로 그의 직업은 succeeded his father이었다. 그는 1278년 스컷랜드에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 소우스 랜아르크실르 벗웰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian nasceu em 1200, em Elginshire, Scotland, seu pai, William de Moravia of Petty, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Olifard, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Devergulla Olifard aproximadamente 1217, em Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian родился в 1200 г. в местности элгиншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени William de Moravia of Petty было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Olifard, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Devergulla Olifard около 1217 г. в местности ботвелл, соут ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. В 1226 году, в возрасте 26 лет,  он работал по профессии succeeded his father. Он умер в 1278 г. в местности Шотландия в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности ботвелл, соут ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Walter de Moray - Lord of Petty, Justiciar of Lothian nació en 1200, en Elginshire, Scotland, su papá, William de Moravia of Petty, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Agnes Olifard, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Devergulla Olifard aproximadamente 1217, en Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. En 1226, a la edad de 26 años, su ocupación registrada era succeeded his father. Murió en 1278, en Scotland, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Bothwell, South Lanarkshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
